data.title: Nuvini Group promotes Gustavo Usero to COO
data.description:
• Nuvini Group (NASDAQ:NVNI) said on Friday that Gustavo Usero has been promoted to Chief Operating Officer, effective September 1st, 2025.
  • Usero currently serves as Group Operating Director at Nuvini, where he leads operational excellence, financial performance, and strategic execution across the group’s portfolio. 
  • In his new role as COO, Usero will continue these efforts while leading the expansion of operations across Latin America.

data.title: Netgear initiated with bullish views at Stifel
data.description:
Stifel began coverage of connectivity solutions provider Netgear (NASDAQ:NTGR) with a Buy rating.

Shares of Netgear rose about 1% premarket on Friday.

Analysts led by Tore Svanberg highlighted three key investment points.

Firstly, the analysts said that the company has a differentiated ProAV platform. Netgear's Business segment (NFB) is becoming the primary growth driver, driven by differentiated ProAV (AV-over-IP) platform, expanded broadcasting and channel partnerships, and broadened solution portfolio that now integrates networking and security through a recent Exium acquisition. It is positioned for double-digit revenue growth and margin expansion in the near-to-medium-term, creating achievable pathway for Netgear to return to profitability in 2027, according to the analysts.

Secondly, there are home networking growth opportunities. The analysts noted that the segment is undergoing a product and strategic pivot designed to stabilize revenue, expand gross margin, and position the company as a trusted, U.S.-based alternative. The analysts expect the Home Networking segment to remain fairly steady in the coming quarters, potentially delivering slight year-over-year growth in 2026.

Lastly, Svanberg and his team highlighted enhanced subscription services. "Perhaps the most structurally transformative element of NETGEAR’s strategy lies in the scaling of its subscription services; specifically, (i) Insight for SMB network management; and (ii) Armor for consumer cybersecurity," said the analysts.

The analysts added that subscription revenue has consistently shown quarter-over-quarter growth, with Insight (cloud -based network management platform) offering centralized configuration, monitoring, and security for Managed Service Providers, or MSPs and small and medium-sized businesses, or SMBs.

Svanberg and his team noted that Armor provides router-level protection for up to 50 devices per household, VPN, ad-blocking, and extended warranty features, creating multiple layers of value within a single subscription.

data.title: AirNet Technology announces $180M registered direct offering
data.description:
• AirNet Technology (NASDAQ:ANTE) said on Friday that it has entered into a definitive agreement with investors for the purchase and sale of 80.8M of its ordinary shares, par value US$0.04 each, and accompanying warrants at a combined purchase price of $2.227 per ordinary share and accompanying warrant in a registered direct offering. 
  • The warrants are immediately exercisable at an exercise price of $3.3405 per ordinary share and will expire five years from the date of issuance. 
  • The gross proceeds to AirNet from the offering are expected to be approximately $180M, before deducting offering expenses payable by the company. 
  • AirNet intends to use the net proceeds from the proposed offering for general corporate and working capital purposes.

data.title: Akanda announces reverse stock split
data.description:

  • AKAN +2.24% after hours to $1.3394.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: JPMorgan Chase to pay Malaysia $330M to settle 1MDB case
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) agreed to pay Malaysia 1.4B ringgit ($330M) to settle all claims relating to 1MDB, the sovereign wealth fund that was the focus of one of the world's largest financial frauds, the government of Malaysia said Friday.

The settlement will end the U.S. lender's exposure to the scandal that came to light in 2015. In 2020, Goldman Sachs (GS) reached a $3.9B settlement with Malaysia to resolve claims that it misled investors in bond sales that raised $6.5B for the fund. The U.S. Department of Justice has determined that $4.5B was misappropriated from 1MDB. 

Also on Friday, Switzerland's Attorney General fined the JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) CHF 3M ($3.7M) for "failing to take all reasonable and necessary organizational measures" to prevent money laundering of the funds misappropriated from 1MDB.

In 2021, 1MDB sued units of JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM), Deutsche Bank (DB), and Coutts & Co., alleging "negligence, breach of contract, conspiracy to defraud/injure, and/or dishonest assistance," and sought to recover alleged losses.

Under the Malaysian agreement, JPMorgan (JPM) will, "without admission of liability,"  pay the amount to Malaysia's Assets Recovery Trust Account.

A JPMorgan spokesman commented, "We appreciate the collaboration with the Malaysian Government in resolving past matters related to 1MDB, which have been thoroughly reviewed. Since then, we’ve enhanced our controls, earning the trust of regulators in Switzerland and beyond. We are pleased to have these matters resolved and remain focused on delivering exceptional service to our clients."

Billions of dollars were funneled out of 1MDB starting in 2009, until the scandal was reported in 2015.

data.title: MCF Energy launches $500,000 private placement
data.description:
• MCF Energy (TSXV:MCF:CA) has announced a non-brokered private placement for aggregate minimum gross proceeds of $500,000.
  • The offering will consist of a minimum of 10M units at a price of $0.05 per unit.  Each unit will consist of one common share and one full common share purchase warrant, with each warrant entitling the holder to acquire an additional common share at an exercise price of $0.06 for a period of 24 months from the date of issuance.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Air Liquide to buy South Korea's DIG Airgas in $3.3B deal
data.description:
Air Liquide (OTCPK:AIQUF) (OTCPK:AIQUY) said Friday it agreed to acquire South Korea's DIG Airgas from Macquarie Asia-Pacific Infrastructure Fund 2 for €2.85B (~$3.3B) including debt.

The French company said the acquisition would strengthen its position in South Korea, the world’s fourth largest industrial gas market and sixth largest manufacturing country by GDP.

DIG reported revenues of €510M in 2024, has nearly 550 employees and 60 plants in South Korea, with operations in all major industrial areas, according to Air Liquide (OTCPK:AIQUF) (OTCPK:AIQUY).

The company said the acquisition represents a multiple of 20.2x EBITDA but falls to 14.8x when DIG's backlog of signed projects and expected cost synergies are taken into account, and decreases even more "when considering other growth synergies that will be generated by this very complementary acquisition."

The deal's indicated multiple was high relative to other valuations in the industry, but it still represents a "minor strategic positive" for Air Liquide (OTCPK:AIQUF) (OTCPK:AIQUY) as M&A opportunities in the highly consolidated industrial gases sector are limited, Jefferies analysts said in a note.

The deal would be Air Liquide's (OTCPK:AIQUF) (OTCPK:AIQUY) largest acquisition since the $13B purchase of Airgas a decade ago.

data.title: Cineplex renews share buyback program
data.description:
• Cineplex (TSX:CGX:CA) has received TSX approval to renew its normal course issuer bid (NCIB).
  • The company may repurchase up to 6.29M common shares, representing 10% of its public float.
  • As of August 14, 2025, Cineplex had 63.44M total shares outstanding.
  • Cineplex may begin purchases on or around August 26, 2025, with the program ending August 25, 2026.
  • Shares bought under the NCIB will be cancelled after purchase.
  • The buyback will be funded using Cineplex’s available cash.
  • Source: Press release
